data.name: Principal Software Engineering Manager
data.jobDescription:
Overview

Azure Core is one of Microsoft Azure’s most impactful and rapidly expanding business areas. The Azure Core Compute team in the India Development Center (IDC) plays a key role in delivering a cloud platform capable of supporting a wide range of workloads. These include major Microsoft services such as Microsoft Teams, Bing, Exchange, Xbox, Structured Query Language (SQL) Azure, Microsoft 365, HDInsight, and Cosmos Database (DB), as well as customers like Accenture, Adobe, Walgreens, Walmart, Flipkart, and InMobi.

The infrastructure developed by the team supports organizations across industries including retail, banking, finance, gaming, logistics, and data analytics. The Azure Resource Mover team focuses on enabling customers to move their Microsoft Azure application resources across regions. This includes building a platform that Azure resource providers can use generically, and supporting resource moves triggered by the underlying Azure platform.

The team is responsible for delivering a seamless customer experience and supporting resource moves at scale. This capability helps customers align with new region launches, access region-specific services, meet data residency requirements, and respond to business developments. It also supports internal platform needs such as capacity optimization and region decommissioning. As the team continues to grow rapidly, it offers a dynamic environment with opportunities to solve complex technical and business challenges.

The team is seeking a customer-focused, engineering-driven Software Engineering Manager to help shape the future of regional resource move scenarios and contribute to the foundational platform. This role is central to evolving the Azure Resource Mover and delivering impactful solutions to customers worldwide. Microsoft's mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.



Responsibilities

- Develop an understanding of customer requirements for their applications and the underlying Azure resources and build a seamless experience to support their cross regional Move in Azure.

- Contribute towards building the platform that makes it possible for generically moving Azure resources cross-region.

- Develop an understanding of platform-initiated Move workflows and support Resource Moves for scenarios triggered by the underlying Azure platform.

- Create long-term vision and roadmap for the team simplifying the overall experience and generically supporting at-scale resource moves.

- Establish KPIs and successfully drive execution through collaboration and partnership with PM team and partners. 

- Lead the team by bringing clarity, creating energy and driving results with an iterative and measurable approach and prioritize work for your team through collaboration with partner teams.

- Accountability for design, implementation, quality, livesite & success of customers and the platform.



Qualifications

Required Qualifications: 

- Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, or related technical discipline AND 10-14 years technical engineering experience with coding in languages including, but not limited to, C, C++, C#, Java, JavaScript, or Python OR equivalent experience.

- Excellent communication skills and passion for managing a diverse team of engineers and helping them grow and proven track record of attracting, hiring, growing, and retaining top talent and working with distributed teams. 

- 2+ years as an engineering manager with experience in technical leadership in driving engineering roadmaps, leading v-teams, mentoring, helping others grow technically and experience/knowledge of cloud computing, enterprise computing, IaaS and/or SaaS product

Other Requirements:   

- Ability to meet Microsoft, customer and/or government security screening requirements are required for this role. These requirements include, but are not limited to the following specialized security screenings:

- Microsoft Cloud Background Check: This position will be required to pass the Microsoft Cloud Background Check upon hire/transfer and every two years thereafter.  

Preferred Qualifications:

- Hands on experience with distributed systems and services in the cloud.

- Experience with public cloud services including Azure, AWS or Google Cloud.

#Azurecore #Azurecompute


This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.



Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance with religious accommodations and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process, read more about requesting accommodations.

blogs.title: Microsoft Sovereign Cloud adds governance, productivity and support for large AI models securely ru
blogs.description: As digital sovereignty becomes a strategic requirement, organizations are rethinking how they deploy critical infrastructure and AI capabilities under tighter regulatory expectations and higher risk conditions. Microsoft’s approach to sovereignty is grounded in enabling enterprises, public sectors and regulated industries to participate in the digital economy securely, independently and on their own terms. The Microsoft Sovereign Cloud brings together productivity, security and cloud workloads to span both... The post  Microsoft Sovereign Cloud adds governance, productivity and support for large AI models securely running even when completely disconnected   appeared first on  The Official Microsoft Blog .

blogs.description: Today, we’re proud to introduce Maia 200, a breakthrough inference accelerator engineered to dramatically improve the economics of AI token generation. Maia 200 is an AI inference powerhouse: an accelerator built on TSMC’s 3nm process with native FP8/FP4 tensor cores, a redesigned memory system with 216GB HBM3e at 7 TB/s and 272MB of on-chip SRAM, plus...
